intake.service_description: App-based rideshare driving for transportation network company
intake.behavioral_control_signals: App routes pickups; driver chooses hours
intake.financial_control_signals: Vehicle leased through platform; driver bears fuel and maintenance

task.issue: Federal common-law worker classification + California ABC test under Cal. Lab. Code §2775

analysis: Federal three-category test: financial control mixed (vehicle through platform vs. fuel by driver); behavioral control elevated (app routes pickups). California ABC test under Cal. Lab. Code §2775: Prong B likely fails because driving-rideshare is the usual course of the platform's business. Note Prop 22 (Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code §7448-7467) carve-out for app-based drivers - currently in litigation; status to verify per CA per-state file.
conclusion: Federal: contractor with §530 plausibility (industry practice prong supports). State (CA): employee for state employment-tax / wage-hour purposes if Prop 22 challenged; otherwise per Prop 22 carve-out app-based driver. Two distinct conclusions: federal vs state.
band_rationale: State conclusion depends on CA per-state file (status: stub at v1.0.0-beta) and Prop 22 ongoing litigation; cap at LOW. Federal conclusion is MODERATE under common-law test but band reduced due to state interplay.
penalty_exposure: Reclassification exposure: §530 federal-only protection; CA state employment-tax exposure if Prop 22 invalidated. Form 8275 not relevant for classification decision.

authorities.citation: Dynamex Operations W. Inc. v. Superior Court, 4 Cal. 5th 903 (2018)
§ 530 of the Revenue Act of 1978 (Pub. L. 95-600, §530, 92 Stat. 2885)
